name: Fatima bint Ali
title: Sermon-Delivering Narrator
shortDescription: The brave daughter of Amir al-Mu'minin (AS) who was present in Karbala and disgraced the Yazidis by delivering a crushing sermon in Kufa.
fullStory:
Fatima bint Ali was the brave and knowledgeable daughter of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS) and one of the devoted sisters of Imam Hussain (AS). From Medina to Mecca and Karbala, she accompanied her brother and her sister Zaynab (SA) and was present in all the tragedies of Ashura.

After the event of Ashura, when the caravan of captives was brought into Kufa in a tragic state, Fatima bint Ali, alongside Lady Zaynab (SA) and Imam Sajjad (AS), broke the deadly silence of Kufa.

She delivered a very crushing and eloquent sermon among the Kufans. In her words, while recounting the virtues of the Ahl al-Bayt, she severely rebuked the Kufans for breaking their covenant and killing the Prophet's son. Her sermon was so impactful that the wailing and crying of the Kufans rose high.

In the court of Sham, too, when a Syrian man asked Yazid to give him Fatima as a slave, Fatima took refuge in Lady Zaynab (SA), and with her sister's brave stand, Yazid and the Syrian man were severely humiliated.
quoteArabic: الحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ عَدَدَ الرَّمْلِ وَالحَصى... يِلْوَيْلِكُمْ! أَتَدْرُونَ أَيَّ يَدٍ طاعَنَتْنا مِنْكُمْ...
quote: (In the Kufa sermon:) Woe unto you! Do you know which hand of yours fought against us?... Your hearts have hardened and your livers turned to stone.
